title: Removal and installation of brake drum

blocks.text: Removal and installation of brake drum

items: Do not allow oil or grease to adhere to the sliding surfaces of the brake lining and brake drum.

items.text: If the brake drum does not come off easily, use a flat head screwdriver to rotate the adjuster bolt (B) through the plug hole to retract the brake shoe.
If the brake drum does not come off easily, use a flat head screwdriver to rotate the adjuster bolt (B) through the plug hole to retract the brake shoe.
If the brake drum is stuck due to rust, etc. and is difficult to remove, tighten the two 8mm bolts (C) evenly at 90° intervals to remove it.
If the brake drum is stuck due to rust, etc. and is difficult to remove, tighten the two 8mm bolts (C) evenly at 90° intervals to remove it.

blocks.text: Installation is the reverse of the removal procedure.

items: If the brake drum is removed by retracting the brake shoe, depress the brake pedal 10 or more times during installation and check that there is no change in pedal stroke.
Clean the mating surfaces of the brake drum and hub bearing unit during installation.
